_about: MCP v2 manual-validation fixtures (subscribe / explain / recommend). One JSON-RPC request + expected response shape per new tool. The expected_response_shape is the schema agents/clients should expect; concrete values vary with the live registry. Use with curl + the deployed Worker at https://hlido.eu/mcp or with the Python server via stdio.

fixtures.scenario: Subscribe to verdict changes for an existing slug; default rss channel.

expected_payload_shape.suggestion: <string explaining Wave 3 deferral and polling alternatives>
expected_payload_shape.channel_advised: <URL — RSS or attestation JSON depending on channel>

notes: Pass channel: 'json' to get /changelog/feed.json; 'webhook' returns the per-slug attestation URL as a polling target.
If slug is not in the registry, returns ok:false with suggestion to use submit_agent or request_quick_audit.

fixtures.scenario: Explain why an agent has its current score, claim by claim, no dimension filter.

expected_payload_shape.summary: <natural-language synthesis: name, score, tier, pass/fail counts, headline>

notes: evidence_quote is truncated to 240 chars per spec.
Pass dimension: 'homepage' to filter to homepage-source claims. Unknown dimensions return supported_dimensions in the response so callers can self-correct.

fixtures.scenario: Buyer wants Coding agents that score 70+ for multi-file refactor work.
notes: category match is case-insensitive equality against registry's `category` field.
tier is treated as a floor (e.g. STEADY admits VITAL+STEADY, not FADING/FLATLINE).
Without use_case, results are ranked purely by Laddoo score desc.
